title: Xeris Crispy Greek Grain Pilaf
description: A resinous, crunchy, pescatarian-friendly Greek grain pilaf featuring mixed grains and a flavorful citrus-herb reduction.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the long-grain rice, bulgur, kamut, dried oregano, and black pepper.
Stir for 2-3 minutes, until the rice and grains are coated in oil and lightly toasted.
Add the lemon juice, tomato paste, and 300ml of water.
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover.
Simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the grains are tender.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and stir in the crumbled feta cheese and chopped parsley.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Reduce heat to low and cook, stirring constantly, until the feta cheese is melted and the pilaf is crispy.
